slides.title:
Introducing
[Product Name]
slides.tagline: [One-line teaser — create tension, not clarity. e.g. 'The last tool you'll ever need for X.']
slides.notes: Teaser slide: build suspense. Don't show the product yet. Let the audience lean in. Use music/silence for effect if presenting live. Keep the product name visible but don't explain it yet.

slides.title: [The pain your users live with — framed as a human story, not a statistic]
slides.user_story: [Describe a typical day in the life of your target user experiencing this pain. Be specific — what do they do, what do they have to work around, what do they silently wish was different?]
slides.notes: Problem slide must feel personal. If you can use a quote from a real user describing their frustration, do it. Statistics anchor the problem in reality — lead with the human story, close with the number.

slides.title: [Product Name]
slides.tagline: [Now explain what it is — one sentence. Crystal clear. No jargon.]
slides.visual_hint: [Describe the hero visual: product screenshot, device mockup, or key UI moment]
slides.notes: The Reveal slide is your 'one more thing' moment. Pause before this slide. Let the product name sit. The visual should be your most polished screenshot or illustration. No bullet points here.

slides.title: Built for [customer type]

items.title: [Feature 1 name — verb-led]
items.desc: [What it does + the outcome. e.g. 'Automate the report that used to take 3 hours. Get it in 30 seconds.']

items.title: [Feature 2 name]
items.desc: [The insight or mechanism that makes this possible. Mention the result users get, not just the method.]

items.title: [Feature 3 name]
items.desc: [The 'wait, it does that too?' moment — the delightful unexpected capability.]

items.title: [Feature 4 name]
items.desc: [Integration or ecosystem play — how it fits into the customer's existing workflow.]

slides.notes: Four features is the sweet spot. More than 5 and nothing is remembered. Each feature card should answer: what does it do + why does it matter to the user's day. Avoid technical jargon.

slides.title: The numbers speak

stats.label: [Secondary impact metric]
stats.context: [From whose data? Beta users? Internal testing?]

slides.notes: Impact slide needs context. '10× faster' than what? Always include the baseline and the source. Beta data is fine — just label it. If you have a before/after screenshot, this is the place.

slides.title: From our beta users

quotes.quote: [Most powerful user quote — ideally shows a measurable outcome or emotional transformation]
quotes.author: [Name, Role @ Company]

quotes.quote: [Second quote — addresses a different use case or persona]
quotes.author: [Name, Role @ Company]

slides.notes: Two strong quotes beat ten mediocre ones. Pick quotes that answer an objection or describe a specific outcome. Avoid generic praise like 'This product is amazing.' Specific is credible.

slides.title: Simple, transparent pricing
slides.notes: Pricing slide: highlight the recommended tier. The Free tier removes the barrier to try. Enterprise 'Contact us' is fine but give them a starting number or budget expectation. Never more than 3 tiers.

slides.title:
Start building with
[Product Name]
slides.notes: Closing slide is a CTA, not a summary. Single most important action you want them to take. Short URL they can actually type. If this is a live event, put the signup URL on screen now and give people 30 seconds to open it.
